(by
    -- Prove that the set {x : Set ι | x ⊆ u} is measurable.
    have h : IsClosed {x : Set ι | x ⊆ u} := by
      -- Step 1: Show that {x : Set ι | x ⊆ u} is closed.
      have h₁ : {x : Set ι | x ⊆ u} = ⋂ (a : ι) (ha : a ∉ u), {x : Set ι | a ∉ x} := by
        apply Set.ext
        intro x
        simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq, Set.mem_inter_iff]
        constructor
        · intro hx a ha
          simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hx ⊢
          intro hxa
          have := hx hxa
          tauto
        · intro hx
          simp only [Set.mem_setOf_eq] at hx ⊢
          intro a ha
          specialize hx a ha
          simp_all
      rw [h₁]
      -- Step 2: Show that the intersection of closed sets is closed.
      apply isClosed_iInter
      intro a
      apply isClosed_iInter
      intro ha
      -- Step 3: Each set {x : Set ι | a ∉ x} is closed.
      have h₂ : IsClosed {x : Set ι | a ∉ x} := by
        have h₃ : IsOpen {x : Set ι | a ∈ x} := by
          apply isOpen_mem
        exact h₃.isClosed_compl
      exact h₂
    exact h.measurableSet
  )

(by
    -- Prove that the predicate `X ω ⊆ u` is measurable
    have h : Measurable (fun x : Set ι => x ⊆ u) := by
      -- Use the fact that the set of subsets not contained in `u` is measurable
      have h₁ : {x : Set ι | ¬x ⊆ u} = ⋃ (i : ι) (hi : i ∈ x), {x : Set ι | i ∈ x ∧ i ∉ u} := by
        -- Prove that the union of certain sets equals the set of subsets not contained in `u`
        ext x
        simp only [Set.mem_iUnion, Set.mem_setOf_eq]
        <;>
        by_cases hx : x ⊆ u <;>
        simp_all [Set.subset_def] <;>
        (try { aesop }) <;>
        (try {
          obtain ⟨i, hi, hni⟩ := hx
          refine' ⟨i, hi, _⟩
          aesop
        }) <;>
        (try {
          tauto
        })
      -- Use the fact that the union of measurable sets is measurable
      have h₂ : Measurable {x : Set ι | ¬x ⊆ u} := by
        rw [h₁]
        -- Use the fact that countable unions of measurable sets are measurable
        apply Measurable.iUnion
        intro i
        apply Measurable.iUnion
        intro hi
        -- Prove that each set in the union is measurable
        simpa [Set.subset_def] using measurable_setOf ‹_›
      -- Use the fact that the complement of a measurable set is measurable
      have h₃ : Measurable {x : Set ι | x ⊆ u} := by
        have h₄ : {x : Set ι | x ⊆ u} = {x : Set ι | ¬x ⊆ u}ᶜ := by
          ext x
          simp
        rw [h₄]
        exact h₂.compl
      exact h₃
    -- Prove that the predicate `X ω ⊆ u` is measurable
    have h₁ : Measurable (fun ω : Ω => X ω ⊆ u) := by
      have h₂ : Measurable (fun x : Set ι => x ⊆ u) := h
      exact h₂.comp (hX.aemeasurable)
    exact h₁
  )
